confirmed_prior_findings.notes: Code: units.py:821-839, gated `if PER_CELL and PC_ENVELOP_PATH and 'envelop' in self.instructions and enemy_cells` — inside the legacy branch of advance_cells, below the units.py:719-720 early return (`if PC_NODE_COHESION and hasattr(self,'_node_pos'): return self._node_advance(...)`), so unreachable whenever PC_NODE_COHESION=1 (default since ED-1089, config.py:87; every Subunit gets _node_pos at construction via units.py:348-349). _node_advance (units.py:554-678) never reads `instructions`. Empirically confirmed both ways: running the package's own acceptance measurement (validators._envelop_reach) under its documented invocation env (PER_CELL=1, field/node defaults) gives BYTE-IDENTICAL results with PC_ENVELOP_PATH on vs off (detachment stays +4.2 rows IN FRONT of the defender); under FIELD_MOVEMENT=0 PC_NODE_COHESION=0 PER_CELL=1 the on-arm reaches +1.0/-1.7 vs off-arm +4.5 (the maneuver is real on the grid). Also empirically: the exact H4 composition on the default path shows wings converging INWARD (cols 3/15 -> ~9.6/13.0) and never crossing the enemy's row extent. Note the double gate: even on the legacy grid path, envelop needs PER_CELL=1, and PER_CELL defaults '0' (config.py:86) — a bare legacy run doesn't get it either.

Code: units.py:849-861, gated `if PER_CELL and PC_SWEEP and 'sweep' in self.instructions and enemy_cells`, legacy branch only. Empirically: validators._sweep_disp under default field/node env gives 0.0 lateral displacement for BOTH PC_SWEEP on and off (identical); under the legacy grid env, on-arm displaces 3.0 columns vs off-arm 0.1. Dead on default path, live on grid+PER_CELL. Confirmed no partial reimplementation elsewhere: resolve_toi_and_commit / _pair_toi_scale / _effective_reach / _reach_throttle (units.py:1042-1154, 118-154, 85-100, 103-115) consume only positions, reach_for(troop_type), cell facing, and halted_cells — no `instructions`, no `role` (grep-verified across the package: the only movement-side instruction consumers are units.py:727 (kite), 821 (envelop), 849 (sweep)).

confirmed_prior_findings.mechanic: ENVELOPMENT WHEEL (overhang cells wheel to flank)
confirmed_prior_findings.notes: Code: units.py:807-813, gated `if PER_CELL and PC_WHEEL and enemy_cells`, legacy branch only. The node path's 'WHEEL' (units.py:622-639) is a different thing entirely — a body-level FACING rotation of the relational layout toward target_centroid, not a per-cell path steer; it produces no overhang-cell wrap. No counterpart exists in the TOI resolve. Additional code-read finding on the node WHEEL itself: its facing update is a lerp-normalize (units.py:632-636, `lr = fr0 + kw*(ftr-fr0)`) that degenerates to a zero vector when the desired facing is exactly anti-parallel to the current facing (kw=disc_mult*0.5=0.5 at full discipline gives lr=lc=0, lmag=0, facing never updates) — i.e. the body-level wheel STALLS at exactly the 180-degree reversal a wheel-to-rear maneuver requires, and axis-aligned mirror deployments make exact anti-parallel facings realistic, not measure-zero. (Code-read confirmed arithmetic; not exercised in a battle probe.)

Confirmed dead on the default path: the kite branch (units.py:727-733, 866) is in the legacy branch below the early return; _node_advance has no standoff-band logic. Empirically: a mounted_archers subunit with explicit unit_type='ranged' + role='Kite' KITES correctly on the legacy grid path (min Chebyshev distance regulated at 4-5 = PC_KITE_STANDOFF band, backing from row 34 to row 44 as the enemy advances) and on the default field path the SAME spec closes to distance 1 and sits in melee for the rest of the battle. CORRECTIONS to the brief: (a) unlike envelop/sweep/wheel, kite is NOT PER_CELL-gated — its gates are PC_KITE_ENABLED (hardcoded True, config.py:111), unit_type=='ranged', the 'kite' instruction, and enemy_cells; so on the grid path it works even with PER_CELL=0. (b) The brief's suspicion about ROLE_SPEC is confirmed and sharper than stated: ROLE_SPEC['Kite'] (config.py:186) sets only shape+instructions, never unit_type; build_army defaults unit_type='melee' (engine.py:216); so NO currently-supported default spec reaches working kite — see new-findings T4 entry. Empirically verified: build_army([{'troop_type':'mounted_archers'}]) yields role='Kite', shape='GappedLine', instructions=('kite','shoot_move'), unit_type='melee' -> kite branch False, volley False.

new_findings.title: reset_positions is a positional no-op on the node/field path — multi-turn re-engagement (the default 'multi' resolving mode) never resets positions
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py:472-473,538-552 (cells() on the node path reads ONLY _node_pos)
tests/sim/mass_battle/bat.py:142-161 (unit_field/cell_field golden digests recorded through this path)
new_findings.evidence: Empirical (default env, exact H4 composition, kind='multi'): battle-turn 1 starts at spawn (a0 centroid (35.0,11.0), b0 (17.2,12.0)); battle-turn 2 tick 1 starts at a0 (27.6,11.5), b0 (24.9,11.1) — exactly where turn 1 ended, mid-field in contact — and turns 3-8 begin at the same locked positions. On the FIELD_MOVEMENT=0/PC_NODE_COHESION=1 combo the same: turns 2-8 (126 of 144 ticks) show ZERO movement, all bodies frozen at the turn-1 contact line. reset_positions' own docstring claims 'Units return to their starting rows for fresh approach' and its 2026-07-02 fix note says it was dogfooded through the multi path — but only the legacy integer-grid state is reset; _node_pos/_node_anchor/_node_rel/_node_facing/_node_facing0/_speed_accum are never touched, and the node path ignores starting_position/cell_offsets entirely.
new_findings.root_cause: reset_positions predates the node path and was never taught about node state. Since ED-1089 flipped PC_NODE_COHESION/FIELD_MOVEMENT default ON, every default 'multi'/'multi_unit' battle (bat.py field digests, gauge_mb multi mode, the workbench) silently runs with no between-turn disengagement/re-approach: after turn 1, all remaining turns are a static grind at the contact line (pre-contact halt at orchestration.py:1074-1109 re-freezes contacting cells every tick). The unit_field/cell_field golden digests embed this behavior, so the fix requires a deliberate field-digest re-record. Note reset_positions also doesn't re-arm stance/_order_idx/instructions/escort latch/cell_facing_vec on either path — wings released in turn 1 stay released in turns 2-8 (possibly intended, but undocumented).

new_findings.title: T4/ED-1093 mounted-archers ruling is NOT effective: the implicit 'Kite' role yields unit_type='melee', so the unit neither kites nor even volleys — it closes to melee, on every path
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/config.py:186 (ROLE_SPEC['Kite'] sets shape+instructions only, no unit_type)
new_findings.evidence: Empirically: build_army([{'troop_type':'mounted_archers'}]) -> role='Kite', shape='GappedLine', instructions=('kite','shoot_move'), unit_type='melee'. Kite-branch precondition evaluates False; volley precondition evaluates False. On the default field path, even an explicitly-fixed spec (unit_type='ranged') closes to melee distance 1 by tick 8 and stays (kite logic absent from _node_advance); on the legacy grid path the explicit spec does kite correctly (distance held at 4-5). So Jordan's ruling ('mounted archers should be flying by and taking advantage of their range advantage') is triply unmet by the shipped default: no bow fire (melee unit_type), no kiting (melee unit_type + node path has no kite logic), and no speed advantage on a bare run (the mounted_archers speed multiplier is PER_CELL-gated, PER_CELL defaults '0' at config.py:86). Also mounted_archers has no TROOP_TYPE_STATS preset (registry.py:34-45), so it silently inherits generic unit stats.
new_findings.root_cause: ROLE_SPEC entries carry no unit_type field, and build_army never derives unit_type from the role; the ED-1093 patch wired only the role/instructions half of the chain and was verified by construction-inspection, not by running the behavior. Fix routing note for Sonnet: per the guardrails, do not special-case mounted_archers in build_army — the declared-I/O-compatible shape is a role-level unit_type (or troop-type-level ranged flag) in the ROLE_SPEC/registry data, Jordan-vetoable.

new_findings.title: v12 column-local targeting is silently reverted on the node path — the default path is a pure centroid attractor, so wings/gapped formations converge inward
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py:789-801 (legacy: per-cell column-local cell_target, the v12 mechanism)
tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py:99-104 (v12 header: 'Replaces centroid-attractor convergence... GappedLine blocks no longer converge... Horseshoe wings don't drift inward')
new_findings.evidence: Empirical H4 default-path run: wings deployed at cols 3 and 15 end at centroid cols ~9.6-9.8 and ~12.4-13.0 — converged onto the enemy centroid's column. The identical composition on the legacy grid keeps wing files (cols 0..7 and 15..25). The node path's only lateral term is the cohesion relax toward anchor+rotated-rel (units.py:653-658), which preserves formation SHAPE around a converging anchor but not deployment files.
new_findings.root_cause: _node_advance moves the whole body's anchor along the single body-level vector to target_centroid; there is no per-cell file-holding term and no per-subunit lateral doctrine. The v12 design decision (documented as a deliberate replacement of centroid-attraction) exists only in the legacy branch, so the default path regressed to pre-v12 convergence behavior. This is also why Jordan sees 'the wings just go straight to the opponent' — straight AND inward.

new_findings.title: The envelop/sweep acceptance validators are dead-armed under their own documented invocation since the ED-1089 default flip, and are wired into no CI
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/validators.py:24 ('Run: PER_CELL=1 PYTHONHASHSEED=0 python3 -m mass_battle.validators' — FIELD/NODE left at ambient default, now ON)
tests/valoria/test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py:18-26 (only the toggle-OFF grid digests are CI-checked; field digests manual-only)
new_findings.evidence: Running validators._envelop_reach/_sweep_disp under the documented env: PC_ENVELOP_PATH on vs off produce identical outputs ([4.2,4.2,4.2,4.2,3.5,4.2] both arms), PC_SWEEP on vs off both all-zero — the maneuver toggles have zero effect because the run executes on the node path. Under FIELD_MOVEMENT=0 PC_NODE_COHESION=0 the arms separate cleanly (envelop 1.0/-1.7 vs 4.5; sweep 3.0 vs 0.1). grep confirms mass_battle.validators is invoked by nothing in CI or tools/.
new_findings.root_cause: The validators pin PER_CELL but not FIELD_MOVEMENT/PC_NODE_COHESION; when ED-1089 flipped the defaults, the acceptance harness for exactly the mechanics that died on the new default started measuring the dead path — so 'Stage C.4's acceptance test passed' claims about envelop are true only of the legacy grid. Any fix must pin toggles explicitly in validators (or run both modes) and get the maneuver validators into an executable gate.

new_findings.title: Formation drift (check_drift shape change) corrupts node state: cells teleport to (0,0) and then permanently stack at the anchor
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py:547 (_node_cells: _node_pos.get(cid,(0.0,0.0)) — missing id renders at battlefield corner)
tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py:653,656 (_node_advance: missing rel defaults (0,0); missing pos setdefaults to the anchor — cells collapse onto one point)
new_findings.evidence: Empirical probe (default env): build Arrowhead tier 3, set discipline 2 (< MIN_DISCIPLINE['Arrowhead']=4, config.py:63-69), call unit.check_drift(). shape becomes 'Line'; cells() then returns (0,0) for 10 of 25 cells — the 10 Line-pattern ids absent from the Arrowhead-keyed _node_pos. On the next _node_advance those ids would be seeded AT the anchor with rel (0,0), i.e. 10 cells permanently stacked on one point.
new_findings.root_cause: ED-1032 fixed drift's cell_troops re-keying for the grid path but the node state (_node_pos/_node_rel, keyed by pattern ids) was never included; _oriented(new shape) yields a different id set. Any battle on the default path in which a non-Line subunit's discipline degrades below its shape minimum (Arrowhead<4, GappedLine<5, Column<3 — reachable via discipline_check_phase degradation) hits this: contact, targeting, volley range, and rendering all read the corrupted cells().

new_findings.title: The per-cell combat/maneuver layer is OFF on a bare default run: PER_CELL defaults '0', so charge shock, brace recoil, cavalry speed, fatigue and envelopment-sigma are all inert in the configuration the workbench visualizes
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py:694-756 (charge_pen depth-absorption, _charge_shock_sigma, envelopment shock, and the ED-1091/ED-1093 brace-recoil gates all nested under `if PER_CELL:`)
tests/sim/mass_battle/workbench/server.py:5-13,79-83 (bare server run = field mode; C2 'braced repel' and C4 'cavalry envelop' presets presuppose the PER_CELL layer)
new_findings.evidence: config.py:86 defaults PER_CELL '0'; the ED-1089 flip changed only FIELD_MOVEMENT/PC_NODE_COHESION. Code read: every charge/brace/recoil/fatigue term in resolve_engagements is inside `if PER_CELL:`; the node path's only PER_CELL consult is the cavalry speed multiplier (units.py:590). So on a bare `python server.py` run — the tick-by-tick visualization Jordan watches — cavalry moves at infantry speed and the C2 preset's namesake recoil cannot fire. The ED-1091/ED-1093 recoil gates are therefore also invisible in that configuration.
new_findings.root_cause: Layer-toggle drift: the field default flip was ratified without deciding PER_CELL's default, leaving the flagship visualization running a configuration in which most role/instruction-adjacent combat mechanics are compiled out. Whether PER_CELL should flip too is a Jordan decision, not a code fix — flagging, not prescribing.

new_findings.title: build_army silently discards unknown per-subunit spec keys — e.g. the 'speed': 'Fast' carried by the C4/workbench cavalry wing specs is dropped without error
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/engine.py:188-222 (pops a fixed key set; leftover keys in sp are ignored)
new_findings.evidence: Code read: build_army pops shape/tier/troop_type/unit_type/starting_position/stance/instructions/role plus the explicit kw loop ('power','discipline','morale','morale_start','dr','stamina','stamina_max','troops','concentration','orders'); anything else ('speed', typos like 'instrucions') is silently lost. 'speed' is Unit-level, so the cavalry wings' Fast pursuit capability in the C4 preset never takes effect and no one is told.
new_findings.root_cause: No unknown-key validation in the spec adapter, contradicting the package's own fail-loud precedent (Order trigger validation, role_allowed). A strict leftover-key check (or explicit per-subunit speed support) is the fix shape.

new_findings.title: Field-path movement speed is direction-dependent: the anchor step is L1-normalized, so diagonal movement is ~29% slower than axis-aligned movement
new_findings.evidence: Code read: the step vector (step*dr/mag, step*dc/mag) has Euclidean length step*sqrt(dr^2+dc^2)/(|dr|+|dc|), which is `step` on an axis but step/sqrt(2)~0.707*step at 45 degrees. On a 'continuous coordinate field' whose distance predicates (TOI, standoff, contact) are Euclidean, movement speed is anisotropic — a flanking arc (exactly the maneuvers under discussion) pays a hidden speed tax versus a frontal advance.
new_findings.root_cause: The L1 normalization was inherited from the grid path's integer step-splitting idiom (units.py:877-880) where it was a rounding scheme, and survived the float migration unexamined.

new_findings.title: 10 of 14 instruction tokens in ROLE_SPEC are consumed nowhere — the roles built from them are pure labels on every path
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py:727,821,849; tests/sim/mass_battle/core/contact.py:53; tests/sim/mass_battle/resolution.py:77,85 (the complete consumer set: kite, envelop, sweep, brace)
new_findings.evidence: Package-wide grep: only 'kite', 'envelop', 'sweep' (movement, legacy-only) and 'brace' (combat, PER_CELL-gated) are ever read. 'hold' (as an instruction — distinct from stance 'hold'), 'pin', 'advance', 'loose', 'harass', 'screen', 'pursue', 'charge', 'lure', 'volley', 'shoot_move', 'reserve' have zero consumers — notably 'charge' (the Shock role's whole package; charging is actually keyed off momentum + troop_type=='cavalry') and 'reserve' (Support/Reserve roles do nothing). config.py:153-156 does mark the scaffold INERT by design, but ED-907 L3 'wired' role selection without wiring most packages, so a caller choosing Shock/Anvil/Screen/Pursue/Feint gets shape defaults only.
new_findings.root_cause: Instruction-to-primitive modulation (pc_formation_design.md §3.5/§9.1) was never landed; role wiring stopped at construction-time defaults. Not a regression, but it materially widens the 'dead instruction' surface the brief asked to bound.

new_findings.title: check_orders trigger semantics: a 'tick:N' order fires exactly once per battle (not once per engagement turn), because _order_idx never re-arms across run_battle calls
new_findings.evidence: Code read + empirical: in the multi-turn H4 runs the wings' stance transitions hold->balanced exactly once (turn 1 tick 4) and instructions gain 'envelop' permanently; snapshots show 'balanced'/'envelop' for turns 2-8 from tick 1. The brief's expectation that the release order re-fires 'once per engagement turn's tick 4, across 8 turns' is wrong — it cannot, by design of the sequential queue. Because the fired behavior persists, this is currently harmless for build_envelopment, but any order whose intent is per-turn (e.g. re-hold each fresh approach) is inexpressible, and this interacts with the reset gap in finding 1 (nothing re-arms between turns).
new_findings.root_cause: Order queue is a one-shot sequence scoped to the Subunit's lifetime, while triggers are phrased in within-turn tick numbers; the two scopes were never reconciled for multi-turn battles.

diagnosis.freeze_anomaly_explanation: REFUTED as described — the freeze does not reproduce, and I can bound where the report went wrong. What I ran (empirically, not from the description): the exact H4 composition (server.py:75-77 / trace.py __main__: build_envelopment center Line@(34,9), wings Line@(34,3)/(34,15), tick:4 release; B = build_unit('Arrowhead', tier 3, anchor 8)) via workbench.trace.run_traced_battle(kind='multi', max_battle_turns=8), with FIELD_MOVEMENT=0 PC_NODE_COHESION=0 PER_CELL=1 set in os.environ BEFORE import (import-time binding verified), seeds 0 and 1, both with infantry wings (the true H4) and cavalry wings (the trace.py demo variant). Result in every case: 144 position snapshots, winner=draw (that part of the report is true), but SUBSTANTIAL movement on both sides every engagement turn — A subunits traverse rows 34->24/25, B traverses rows 15->35 into contact, hp 1200->~997 and 400->~342. I repeated this at HEAD (7bfa375) and at the three preceding commits that contain the preset machinery (9040c08, 8443b4c, 52ce772) via git-archive extraction: byte-identical row ranges at all four code states. So 'zero movement across all 144 ticks on the legacy path' is not a property of any recent state of this code under the stated toggles. Ruled out beyond the brief's own checks: (a) orders do fire and persist — snapshots show wings hold->balanced + 'envelop' at turn 1 tick 4 (with the correction that the order fires once per battle, not once per turn — contact.py:58; harmless here); (b) assign_targets always yields a target ('nearest' is unconditional, contact.py:73-76), so the moving_a/moving_b gates (orchestration.py:1193-1196) are non-empty; (c) no toggle combination I tested (FIELD/NODE/PER_CELL in {0,1} consistent combos) freezes both sides from their start rows. What remains open is what the prior test actually measured; two concrete mechanisms would produce exactly this false report: (1) SNAPSHOT SCHEMA TRAP — each cell entry in the 'positions' events is {'id': [pattern-coordinate], 'pos': [r,c], ...} (orchestration.py:1017-1025); 'id' is the immutable formation coordinate and is CONSTANT for all 144 ticks; reading id instead of pos (or reading Subunit.starting_position, which on the legacy path never changes intra-battle) reports 'frozen at starting row/column' for every subunit on both sides while the battle moves normally — this matches the reported symptom exactly, including 'both sides' and 'draw'. (2) NODE-COMBO NEAR-MISS — if PC_NODE_COHESION was NOT effectively 0 (set after import, typo'd, or left default), every combination with node cohesion ON produces 126 of 144 ticks with literally zero movement: turn 1 closes to contact, then — because reset_positions never resets _node_pos (new finding 1) and the pre-contact halt re-freezes contacting cells each tick — turns 2-8 are perfectly static (empirically: turn-start centroids identical from turn 2 onward). An observer sampling after turn 1, or aggregating, sees a frozen battle — though at mid-field contact positions, not start rows, so mechanism (1) fits the literal report better. Bottom line for the fixer: there is no legacy-path freeze bug to fix; the real, confirmed pathologies in this neighborhood are the node-path reset no-op and the static turns 2-8 it causes.
diagnosis.capability_architecture_assessment: The current default-path architecture CANNOT express either capability as-is, but it does not need a ground-up rearchitecture — it needs one new primitive (a per-subunit sequence of intermediate goal points with completion predicates) plus three targeted repairs. Why the current model fails: _node_advance steers the anchor along a single vector to one point, target_centroid (units.py:596-616), and every caller feeds it the enemy body's cached centroid (orchestration.py:1126-1129, 1200-1204). With a single enemy-anchored attractor there is no way to move AWAY from the target first (image 1's wide-out leg) — the only distance-increasing mode is stance='retreat', which inverts the entire vector — and no way to aim at a point that is neither an enemy centroid nor an escort slot (image 2's interior strike point). The body WHEEL (units.py:622-639) is facing-only, not path-shaping, and its lerp-normalize facing update degenerates at 180-degree reversals (units.py:634-636) — exactly the wheel-to-rear case. The TOI resolve is goal-agnostic (pure standoff enforcement) and needs no change. Why a waypoint primitive is sufficient AND already half-proven in this codebase: the legacy 'envelop' (units.py:821-839) is precisely a 2-state machine that substitutes a synthetic goal (a point wide of the nearer flank AND beyond the enemy's far row edge, recomputed EVERY tick from live enemy extent) until a geometric predicate flips (_past: my advance-axis coordinate beyond the enemy's row extent — Jordan's 'literally at a smaller x than their opponent' crossing test, already implemented), then re-targets the nearest now-rear cell. Generalizing that to an ordered list of (goal-function, completion-predicate) pairs consumed by _node_advance ahead of the target_centroid fallback expresses both images: image 1 = [wide-flank point, behind-enemy point gated on the crossing predicate, terminal rear-attack]; image 2 = per-wing DIFFERENT lists, where the right wing's terminal goal is a point computed from ALLIED geometry ('between center and left wing' — the Order.waypoint_ref idiom at units.py:211 and the 'ally_at:D' trigger already establish allied-reference goals as a supported pattern). Two design constraints matter: goals must be FUNCTIONS of live geometry re-evaluated per tick (frozen coordinates go stale against a moving enemy — the legacy envelop already does this right), and predicates should reuse the existing Order-trigger vocabulary (tick/enemy_range/ally_at plus a new crossed-past predicate) rather than grow a parallel dialect (shape-divergence guardrail). Concrete changes required: (1) _node_advance gains a goal-resolution step (waypoint-or-centroid) — ~the only edit to the steering core; it must also decide facing policy during transit (WHEEL currently faces target_centroid at units.py:625; during a wide-out leg the body should face its movement goal or the threat, a Jordan-vetoable design choice); (2) fix the WHEEL 180-degree stall (finding under 'wheel'), or rear attacks will arrive with wrong facing; (3) fix reset_positions node-state no-op first — multi-turn maneuvers are meaningless while turns 2+ start welded to the contact line; (4) restore a lateral-doctrine term (v12 column-holding or a formation-frame goal offset) or wide wings will converge inward during the approach leg before the maneuver even begins (finding 3); (5) clearance handling: the pre-contact halt + TOI standoff will freeze a wing that skirts inside contact radius while passing the flank — the waypoint generator must maintain clearance > standoff (the legacy envelop's frontage+2 margin is the precedent), and battlefield clamping (units.py:657-658, BATTLEFIELD_SIZE=50) bounds how wide 'wide' can be, which the goal function must respect rather than emit out-of-bounds points. Discipline notes for the implementer: all of this must live on the node path only (FIELD_MOVEMENT=0 PC_NODE_COHESION=0 byte-exactness is a frozen invariant enforced by CI's grid digests, tests/valoria/test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py); the field digests (bat.py unit_field/cell_field) WILL change and need a deliberate Jordan-flagged re-record; no new magnitudes may be invented (clearance margins etc. should derive from existing ratified quantities — standoff_from_reach, COL_WIDTH, enemy extent — TROOP_TYPE_REACH stays empty); and the maneuver acceptance validators must be re-pointed at the node path (they currently measure the dead arm — finding 4) before any claim that the new capability works.
open_questions: What did the 2026-07-02 freeze test actually measure? The literal 144-tick freeze is refuted at HEAD and the three preceding commits; the snapshot 'id'-vs-'pos' misread is the best-fit explanation but the original probe script was not available to inspect.

Is the multi-turn persistence of released stance/instructions/orders (wings never re-hold after turn 1; _order_idx never re-arms) intended semantics for 'each turn = fresh approach', or should reset_positions/between_turn_recovery re-arm the command state? This needs a Jordan ruling before the reset fix defines what turn-2+ posture is.

Should PER_CELL default flip to '1' alongside ED-1089's field flip? Today the flagship workbench visualization runs with charge shock, brace recoil, cavalry speed, fatigue and the ED-1091/ED-1093 gates all compiled out (PER_CELL=0), which is presumably not what Jordan believes he is watching.

Where should the Kite role acquire unit_type='ranged' — a unit_type field in ROLE_SPEC entries, or a ranged flag on the troop type? Either fixes ED-1093 without special-casing an entity; both are schema changes to ratified data (Jordan-vetoable).

During a waypoint transit leg, what should the body FACE — the movement goal, the enemy, or slew between them phase-by-phase? The current WHEEL conflates steering target and facing target; image 1's arc makes them differ for many ticks.

What re-verification does the reset_positions fix demand: the unit_field/cell_field digests were recorded WITH the broken reset, gauge_mb's multi-mode historical bands were presumably last validated on the grid path — do the field-path gauge bands need a full re-run once turns 2+ actually re-approach?

The pre-existing platform-dependent RNG desync in the 'cell' grid digest (documented in tests/valoria/test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py:32-50) remains unfixed; any re-baselining work in this area will collide with it.

verified.title: ENVELOPMENT WHEEL (overhang cells wheel to flank)

detail.mechanic: ENVELOPMENT WHEEL (overhang cells wheel to flank)
detail.notes: Code: units.py:807-813, gated `if PER_CELL and PC_WHEEL and enemy_cells`, legacy branch only. The node path's 'WHEEL' (units.py:622-639) is a different thing entirely — a body-level FACING rotation of the relational layout toward target_centroid, not a per-cell path steer; it produces no overhang-cell wrap. No counterpart exists in the TOI resolve. Additional code-read finding on the node WHEEL itself: its facing update is a lerp-normalize (units.py:632-636, `lr = fr0 + kw*(ftr-fr0)`) that degenerates to a zero vector when the desired facing is exactly anti-parallel to the current facing (kw=disc_mult*0.5=0.5 at full discipline gives lr=lc=0, lmag=0, facing never updates) — i.e. the body-level wheel STALLS at exactly the 180-degree reversal a wheel-to-rear maneuver requires, and axis-aligned mirror deployments make exact anti-parallel facings realistic, not measure-zero. (Code-read confirmed arithmetic; not exercised in a battle probe.)

verdict.evidence:
I read /home/user/ttrpg/tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py directly and confirmed every code-read claim:

1) ENVELOPMENT WHEEL, units.py:807-813, verbatim gate `if PER_CELL and PC_WHEEL and enemy_cells:` (line 807). It computes enemy column span emin/emax, and for an OVERHANG cell (`my_c < emin or my_c > emax`, line 811) retargets cell_target to the nearest enemy cell by squared distance (lines 812-813). This block lives inside advance_cells (def at 710), which is the LEGACY path: line 719-720 early-returns to _node_advance when `PC_NODE_COHESION and hasattr(self,'_node_pos')`, so 807-813 only runs on the non-node branch. Confirmed "legacy branch only."

2) Node WHEEL, units.py:617-639, is a distinct mechanic: a body-level FACING rotation. It builds a unit direction to target_centroid (ftr,ftc, line 628), slews _node_facing toward it (632-636), then derives rotation (cos rc_w, sin rs_w) f0->f (637-639), which rotates the relational layout (des_r/des_c at 654-655). It is not a per-cell path steer and produces no overhang-cell wrap. Confirmed.

3) TOI resolve counterpart: resolve_toi_and_commit (def at 1042) is purely a cross-side continuous-collision cap+commit; I read 1042-1116 and it only flattens proposals, computes standoff targets, and throttles motion — no overhang/frontage-span detection and no wheel-to-flank retarget. Confirmed no counterpart.

4) Anti-parallel stall arithmetic: node disc_mult = 1.0 for discipline>=5 (line 584), so kw = disc_mult*0.5 = 0.5 (line 632). The lerp lr = fr0 + kw*(ftr-fr0) = 0.5*(fr0+ftr). I ran a probe: at exact anti-parallel (ftr=-fr0) lr=lc=0.0, lmag=0.0, and `if lmag > 0` (line 636) is false so _node_facing never updates. At 90deg (lmag=0.707) and near-anti-parallel (lmag=0.022) it does update. The zero is specific to kw=0.5 (full discipline); at disc 3-4 kw=0.35 gives 0.30*fr0 != 0, no stall. The finding correctly scopes the degeneracy to full discipline.
verdict.correction: No correction to the code facts — all confirmed exact. One caveat, which the finding itself already flags ("Code-read confirmed arithmetic; not exercised in a battle probe"): the claim that "axis-aligned mirror deployments make exact anti-parallel facings realistic, not measure-zero" is asserted, not demonstrated. In continuous float geometry the stall requires ftr to equal -fr0 to full precision, which is a knife-edge (measure-zero) condition and self-correcting on any subsequent tick where geometry shifts; whether it is ever actually hit in a real battle is not shown. So the arithmetic degeneracy is CONFIRMED_EXACT, but its operational reachability/impact remains an untested assertion rather than an observed defect.

detail.notes: Confirmed dead on the default path: the kite branch (units.py:727-733, 866) is in the legacy branch below the early return; _node_advance has no standoff-band logic. Note reset_positions also doesn't re-arm stance/_order_idx/instructions/escort latch/cell_facing_vec on either path — wings released in turn 1 stay released in turns 2-8 (possibly intended, but undocumented).

verdict.evidence: Read the cited code myself: orchestration.py:1444-1469 shows reset_positions writes ONLY starting_position/cell_offsets/cell_offsets_c and never calls _init_node_state() or touches any _node_* field. units.py:471-482 shows cells() short-circuits to _node_cells() whenever PC_NODE_COHESION and hasattr(_node_pos), and _node_cells() (538-552) reads ONLY _node_pos, ignoring starting_position/cell_offsets. _init_node_state() is invoked only in __post_init__ (units.py:349), never between turns; run_battle (1043-1084) does not re-init it. config.py:87 and units.py:31 confirm PC_NODE_COHESION and FIELD_MOVEMENT both default '1' (ON, ED-1089). verified.title: v12 column-local targeting is silently reverted on the node path — the default path is a pure centroid attractor, so wings/gapped formations converge inward

detail.title: v12 column-local targeting is silently reverted on the node path — the default path is a pure centroid attractor, so wings/gapped formations converge inward
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py:789-801 (legacy: per-cell column-local cell_target, the v12 mechanism)
tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py:99-104 (v12 header: 'Replaces centroid-attractor convergence... GappedLine blocks no longer converge... Horseshoe wings don't drift inward')
detail.evidence: Empirical H4 default-path run: wings deployed at cols 3 and 15 end at centroid cols ~9.6-9.8 and ~12.4-13.0 — converged onto the enemy centroid's column. The identical composition on the legacy grid keeps wing files (cols 0..7 and 15..25). The node path's only lateral term is the cohesion relax toward anchor+rotated-rel (units.py:653-658), which preserves formation SHAPE around a converging anchor but not deployment files.
detail.root_cause: _node_advance moves the whole body's anchor along the single body-level vector to target_centroid; there is no per-cell file-holding term and no per-subunit lateral doctrine. The v12 design decision (documented as a deliberate replacement of centroid-attraction) exists only in the legacy branch, so the default path regressed to pre-v12 convergence behavior. This is also why Jordan sees 'the wings just go straight to the opponent' — straight AND inward.

verdict.evidence: I read all cited lines and reproduced the behavior. units.py:595-616: _node_advance sets dr/dc = target_centroid - anchor (both row AND column) and steps the whole subunit anchor straight at target_centroid — a pure centroid attractor with no column-holding, exactly as claimed. units.py:534 shows _node_rel is each cell's fixed offset from its subunit anchor, so lines 653-658 only preserve shape WITHIN a subunit, not deployment files of the anchor. units.py:789-801: the v12 per-cell column-local cell_target=(target_centroid[0], my_starting_col) lives only in the legacy advance_cells branch. advance_cells/_node_advance are Subunit methods (class at 223) called per-subunit in run_battle with cached_centroids[id(atom)] = atom.target_atom.centroid() (orchestration.py:1128-1129,1200-1204), so each wing is a separate anchor that steers its whole body at the enemy subunit centroid. I also confirmed _node_advance (554-678) references NONE of instructions/envelop/sweep/overhang/PC_WHEEL/PC_ENVELOP/PC_SWEEP — so the entire ED-909 envelop/wheel/sweep maneuver battery, not just column targeting, is inert on the default node path. Empirical: I built an envelopment (wings at cols 3 and 15) vs a Line and ran run_battle. Node path (default FIELD_MOVEMENT=1/PC_NODE_COHESION=1): wings converge to cols 9.0 and 13.0 (gap 12->4). Legacy path (both=0): wings hold files at cols 5.0 and 17.0. This reproduces the finding's core evidence. The v40/orchestration header line 102 ('GappedLine blocks no longer converge... Horseshoe wings do not drift inward') describes a mechanism present only in the legacy branch.
verdict.correction: The central, high-severity claim is CONFIRMED: on the default node path the subunit anchor is a pure centroid attractor, v12 column-local targeting (and the whole ED-909 envelop/wheel/sweep battery) is legacy-only, and multi-subunit wings converge inward (empirically 3,15 -> 9.0,13.0 on node vs 5.0,17.0 on legacy). One part of the finding's TITLE overreaches: 'gapped formations converge inward' is FALSE for a single GappedLine subunit. Because the node path preserves each cell's rel offset from its own anchor, a lone GappedLine's internal gap is maintained under rigid translation (probe: deploy cols [9,10,11,12,14,15,16,17] gap@13 -> final [8,9,10,11,13,14,15,16] gap@12 — gap stays open, just shifts one column). Convergence only occurs BETWEEN separate subunit anchors (wings), not within a single gapped subunit. So the regression is real and load-bearing for multi-body compositions (the finding's actual empirical evidence, which is entirely about wings, is fully reproduced), but the incidental generalization to single 'gapped formations' does not hold. Also worth noting for the fix: the deeper regression is that the node path ignores subunit instructions entirely, so the wings' intended 'envelop' wrap-to-rear maneuver never runs on the default path — they go straight at (and inward toward) the enemy centroid, which explains 'the wings just go straight to the opponent.'

Under FIELD_MOVEMENT=0 PC_NODE_COHESION=0 the arms separate cleanly (envelop 1.0/-1.7 vs 4.5; sweep 3.0 vs 0.1). grep confirms mass_battle.validators is invoked by nothing in CI or tools/.
detail.root_cause: The validators pin PER_CELL but not FIELD_MOVEMENT/PC_NODE_COHESION; when ED-1089 flipped the defaults, the acceptance harness for exactly the mechanics that died on the new default started measuring the dead path — so 'Stage C.4's acceptance test passed' claims about envelop are true only of the legacy grid. Any fix must pin toggles explicitly in validators (or run both modes) and get the maneuver validators into an executable gate.

verdict.evidence: I read the cited files and ran my own probe. (1) Defaults are ON post-ED-1089: hierarchy/units.py:31 `FIELD_MOVEMENT = env.get("FIELD_MOVEMENT","1")=="1"` and config.py:87 `PC_NODE_COHESION = env.get('PC_NODE_COHESION','1')=='1'` with an inline `[ED-1089, Jordan-ratified 2026-07-02] DEFAULT FLIPPED 0 -> 1` comment. (2) The validators' documented invocation (validators.py:23 `Run: PER_CELL=1 PYTHONHASHSEED=0 python3 -m mass_battle.validators`) pins PER_CELL only — not the field toggles. Running v_envelop/v_sweep under exactly that env, both arms are byte-identical and both FAIL: V-ENVELOP on_m=4.21 vs off_m=4.21 (0 behind vs 0 behind), V-SWEEP on_m=0.0 vs off_m=0.0, with FIELD_MOVEMENT=True/PC_NODE_COHESION=True. Under FIELD_MOVEMENT=0 PC_NODE_COHESION=0 the same validators separate cleanly and PASS: V-ENVELOP 0.55 vs 4.2 (3 behind vs 0), V-SWEEP 3.0 vs 0.09. (3) Mechanism confirmed by source: advance_cells (units.py:719-720) returns early to _node_advance when PC_NODE_COHESION is on; _node_advance (554-680) contains no `envelop`/`sweep`/`instructions` handling — the maneuver blocks (units.py:821 envelop, 849 sweep) live only in the legacy branch that field-ON never reaches (the code's own note at line 872 states this). (4) No CI wiring: grep for mass_battle.validators / v_envelop / v_sweep / import validators across tools/, .github/, tests/valoria/ and the whole repo returns only the module's own file plus two archived handoff YAMLs; the sole mass_battle test in the CI pytest dir is test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py, whose _PINNED_OFF (lines 74-75) forces every toggle OFF and whose docstring (18-26) states the FIELD_MOVEMENT=1 digests are NOT checked in CI (manual only); the CI workflow runs only `pytest tests/valoria` and `pytest sim/tests`, neither of which invokes the acceptance validators.
verdict.correction: Substance fully holds; two trivial citation nits, neither load-bearing. (a) The documented `Run:` line is validators.py:23, not :24 (24 is the closing docstring line). (b) The finding's specific per-seed grid-oracle numbers ("envelop 1.0/-1.7 vs 4.5; sweep 3.0 vs 0.1") are looser than my reproduced aggregate means (envelop on 0.55 vs off 4.2; sweep 3.0 vs 0.09) — the discrepancy is aggregate-mean vs per-seed reporting and does not affect the conclusion that the arms separate cleanly on the grid and are dead-identical on the field path. The core claim — envelop/sweep validators are dead-armed under their own documented invocation since the ED-1089 flip, and are wired into no CI — is correct on every load-bearing point.

verified.title: Formation drift (check_drift shape change) corrupts node state: cells teleport to (0,0) and then permanently stack at the anchor

detail.title: Formation drift (check_drift shape change) corrupts node state: cells teleport to (0,0) and then permanently stack at the anchor
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py:547 (_node_cells: _node_pos.get(cid,(0.0,0.0)) — missing id renders at battlefield corner)
tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py:653,656 (_node_advance: missing rel defaults (0,0); missing pos setdefaults to the anchor — cells collapse onto one point)
detail.evidence: Empirical probe (default env): build Arrowhead tier 3, set discipline 2 (< MIN_DISCIPLINE['Arrowhead']=4, config.py:63-69), call unit.check_drift(). shape becomes 'Line'; cells() then returns (0,0) for 10 of 25 cells — the 10 Line-pattern ids absent from the Arrowhead-keyed _node_pos. On the next _node_advance those ids would be seeded AT the anchor with rel (0,0), i.e. 10 cells permanently stacked on one point.
detail.root_cause: ED-1032 fixed drift's cell_troops re-keying for the grid path but the node state (_node_pos/_node_rel, keyed by pattern ids) was never included; _oriented(new shape) yields a different id set. Any battle on the default path in which a non-Line subunit's discipline degrades below its shape minimum (Arrowhead<4, GappedLine<5, Column<3 — reachable via discipline_check_phase degradation) hits this: contact, targeting, volley range, and rendering all read the corrupted cells().

verdict.evidence:
I read all three cited code sites and they say exactly what the finding claims:

(1) units.py:1288-1301 — check_drift() on drift does `a.shape="Line"` then rebuilds ONLY `a.cell_troops = {pid: per for pid in new_ids}` where new_ids come from `_oriented(a)` of the new shape. It never touches `_node_pos`/`_node_rel`. The ED-1032 comment (1293-1296) explicitly scopes the fix to cell_troops re-keying.

(2) units.py:547 (_node_cells) — `r,c = self._node_pos.get((orig_r,orig_c),(0.0,0.0))`: any id absent from _node_pos renders at (0,0).

(3) units.py:653 `rel = self._node_rel.get(cid,(0.0,0.0))` and 656 `cr,cc = self._node_pos.setdefault(cid, self._node_anchor)`: an unseen cell is seeded at the anchor with a zero relational offset.

I also confirmed geometry.py:334 — `_oriented` builds the pattern from `su.shape` via footprint_for, so changing Arrowhead→Line yields a different id set, and config.py:63-69 gives MIN_DISCIPLINE['Arrowhead']=4. Defaults: FIELD_MOVEMENT=1, PC_NODE_COHESION=1 (units.py:31, config.py:87) — the finding's "default env".

Empirical probe (default env, tests/sim): built Subunit(shape='Arrowhead',tier=3,discipline=2) wrapped in a Unit, verified cells() gave 25 cells with 0 at (0,0) before drift. After unit.check_drift(): shape→'Line', and cells() returned (0,0) for exactly 10 of 25 cells — precisely the 10 Line-pattern ids missing from the Arrowhead-keyed _node_pos (new_ids − old keys = 10). After one _node_advance(target_centroid=(40,40)): _node_pos grew 25→35 (10 ids setdefault'd to the anchor (11.2,14.0)), all 10 collapsed to the single point (11.2,14.0) with rel offsets all (0,0); after 9 advances they STILL occupy exactly 1 distinct position while the other 15 cells occupy 15 distinct positions — permanent stacking, exactly as claimed.

Reachability confirmed: check_drift is called per-tick in the live loop (orchestration.py:330, 1353; core/state.py:94), immediately after discipline_check_phase calls degrade_discipline (state.py:93), so a spawned-Arrowhead subunit whose discipline degrades below 4 hits this on the default path. Not dead code.
verdict.correction: None needed — the finding is accurate in code citations, empirical evidence, root cause (ED-1032 re-keyed cell_troops but not the node _node_pos/_node_rel state), and reachability. One clarifying nuance: the two corrupt states are sequential, both real — between drift and the next advance the 10 cells render at (0,0) (via _node_cells's (0,0) default); on the first _node_advance they are re-seeded at the anchor and thereafter stay permanently stacked on that one point (relaxing toward anchor+rel(0,0)=anchor). The finding describes exactly this "teleport to (0,0) then stack at anchor" progression. Also note check_drift/cells() are Subunit-level methods and check_drift() is the Unit method iterating subunits, but for a single-subunit unit (the tested case) this distinction doesn't change the result.

verified.title: The per-cell combat/maneuver layer is OFF on a bare default run: PER_CELL defaults '0', so charge shock, brace recoil, cavalry speed, fatigue and envelopment-sigma are all inert in the configuration the workbench visualizes

detail.title: The per-cell combat/maneuver layer is OFF on a bare default run: PER_CELL defaults '0', so charge shock, brace recoil, cavalry speed, fatigue and envelopment-sigma are all inert in the configuration the workbench visualizes
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/orchestration.py:694-756 (charge_pen depth-absorption, _charge_shock_sigma, envelopment shock, and the ED-1091/ED-1093 brace-recoil gates all nested under `if PER_CELL:`)
tests/sim/mass_battle/workbench/server.py:5-13,79-83 (bare server run = field mode; C2 'braced repel' and C4 'cavalry envelop' presets presuppose the PER_CELL layer)
detail.evidence: config.py:86 defaults PER_CELL '0'; the ED-1089 flip changed only FIELD_MOVEMENT/PC_NODE_COHESION. Code read: every charge/brace/recoil/fatigue term in resolve_engagements is inside `if PER_CELL:`; the node path's only PER_CELL consult is the cavalry speed multiplier (units.py:590). So on a bare `python server.py` run — the tick-by-tick visualization Jordan watches — cavalry moves at infantry speed and the C2 preset's namesake recoil cannot fire. The ED-1091/ED-1093 recoil gates are therefore also invisible in that configuration.
detail.root_cause: Layer-toggle drift: the field default flip was ratified without deciding PER_CELL's default, leaving the flagship visualization running a configuration in which most role/instruction-adjacent combat mechanics are compiled out. Whether PER_CELL should flip too is a Jordan decision, not a code fix — flagging, not prescribing.

verdict.evidence:
I read all four cited locations and ran a bare-default import probe.

config.py:86 — `PER_CELL = _sigma_os.environ.get('PER_CELL', '0') == '1'` → defaults OFF. The adjacent line 87 confirms the ED-1089 flip touched only PC_NODE_COHESION ("DEFAULT FLIPPED 0 -> 1 together with FIELD_MOVEMENT"); PER_CELL carries no such flip note. units.py:31 FIELD_MOVEMENT defaults '1'. So the ED-1089 flip changed only FIELD_MOVEMENT/PC_NODE_COHESION, exactly as claimed.

units.py:590 — `if PER_CELL and self.troop_type in ('cavalry','mounted_archers') and step > 0:` applies PC_CAVALRY_SPEED_MULT (2.0). This is the node path's only PER_CELL consult; with PER_CELL off, cavalry uses the same `step` as infantry. Confirmed.

orchestration.py — every charge/maneuver term is nested under `if PER_CELL:`: charge_pen depth-absorption (694-700), _charge_shock_sigma + envelopment shock (709-728), the ED-1091/ED-1093 brace-recoil block (748-756, itself further gated by PC_BRACE_ENABLED but only reachable inside the 709 `if PER_CELL:`), fatigue sigma (763-765), and envelopment sigma (766-769). Line 553 also shows `_pc_refuse = PER_CELL and PC_REFUSE`. Confirmed.

server.py:1-13 — docstring: "A bare run now visualizes the COORDINATE FIELD — FIELD_MOVEMENT/PC_NODE_COHESION default ON since the ED-1089 flip"; it never mentions PER_CELL, and env is read once at import into star-imported value copies. C2 preset (82-83) sets instructions:['brace'] and C4 (79-81) uses cavalry wings — both presuppose the PER_CELL layer.

Empirical probe (bare `python3`, all PER_CELL-family env vars unset): PER_CELL=False, FIELD_MOVEMENT=True, PC_NODE_COHESION=True, PC_BRACE_ENABLED=True, PC_REFUSE=True — and critically the star-imported copies units.PER_CELL=False and orch.PER_CELL=False. So on a bare server run PC_BRACE_ENABLED/PC_REFUSE being True is moot: the outer `if PER_CELL:` short-circuits the whole layer. Cavalry speed mult, charge shock, brace recoil, fatigue, and envelopment-sigma are all compiled out in the visualized config.
verdict.correction: No correction needed — the finding is accurate as stated. Minor clarifications that do NOT contradict it: (1) base puncture/momentum penetration (a_pen/b_pen at 690-693, 701-702) still fires without PER_CELL; only the charge_pen depth-absorption ADDITION is PER_CELL-gated — the finding correctly scopes its claim to "charge_pen depth-absorption," not all puncture. (2) Graded morale sigma (761-762) is NOT PER_CELL-gated and remains live; the finding does not claim otherwise. (3) PC_ENVELOP_SHOCK lives in orchestration.py:473 (default ON), not config.py, but is still nested under the 709 `if PER_CELL:`, so it too is inert on a bare run. The severity framing (a Jordan design decision on whether PER_CELL should also flip, not a code bug) is appropriate.

verified.title: build_army silently discards unknown per-subunit spec keys — e.g. the 'speed': 'Fast' carried by the C4/workbench cavalry wing specs is dropped without error

detail.title: build_army silently discards unknown per-subunit spec keys — e.g. the 'speed': 'Fast' carried by the C4/workbench cavalry wing specs is dropped without error
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/engine.py:188-222 (pops a fixed key set; leftover keys in sp are ignored)
detail.evidence: Code read: build_army pops shape/tier/troop_type/unit_type/starting_position/stance/instructions/role plus the explicit kw loop ('power','discipline','morale','morale_start','dr','stamina','stamina_max','troops','concentration','orders'); anything else ('speed', typos like 'instrucions') is silently lost. 'speed' is Unit-level, so the cavalry wings' Fast pursuit capability in the C4 preset never takes effect and no one is told.
detail.root_cause: No unknown-key validation in the spec adapter, contradicting the package's own fail-loud precedent (Order trigger validation, role_allowed). A strict leftover-key check (or explicit per-subunit speed support) is the fix shape.

verdict.evidence: Read engine.py:188-222: build_army copies each spec dict, pops a fixed key set (starting_position/troop_type/role/shape/tier/instructions/unit_type/stance) plus an explicit kw loop over power/discipline/morale/morale_start/dr/stamina/stamina_max/troops/concentration/orders, then calls Subunit.of_type; leftover keys in sp are never read or validated — no unknown-key check. 'speed' is in neither set. Confirmed server.py:61-62 (_ENVELOP_WINGS_CAV) and trace.py:112-115 both pass per-subunit 'speed':'Fast'. Confirmed via units.py:1159/1182 that speed is a Unit-level dataclass field, and Subunit (units.py:223-351) has no speed field at all. build_envelopment (engine.py:257-264) merges wing_specs into build_army and sets Unit speed only from its own speed= param (default 'Standard'), never from specs. Ran a probe: build_army with a 'speed':'Fast' spec builds with no error and yields Unit.speed=='Standard' with the subunit having no speed attr; a typo key ('instrucions') likewise builds silently; Subunit.of_type(..., speed='Fast') raises TypeError, proving the fixed-key pop is precisely what avoids a crash by dropping the key. Also verified the fail-loud precedent the finding cites is real: an invalid role raises ValueError via role_allowed (engine.py:203-205).
verdict.correction: Finding is accurate. Minor strengthening only: the impact is understated — 'speed' is not merely dropped by the adapter but is unrepresentable at subunit granularity (Subunit has no speed field; speed exists solely on Unit), so the per-wing Fast pursuit has no home in the data model even if the adapter forwarded it. The C4 wings' Fast is therefore lost end-to-end, and the composed Unit's speed also stays 'Standard' because build_envelopment's speed param defaults 'Standard' and is never fed from the specs.

verified.title: Field-path movement speed is direction-dependent: the anchor step is L1-normalized, so diagonal movement is ~29% slower than axis-aligned movement

detail.title: Field-path movement speed is direction-dependent: the anchor step is L1-normalized, so diagonal movement is ~29% slower than axis-aligned movement
detail.evidence: Code read: the step vector (step*dr/mag, step*dc/mag) has Euclidean length step*sqrt(dr^2+dc^2)/(|dr|+|dc|), which is `step` on an axis but step/sqrt(2)~0.707*step at 45 degrees. On a 'continuous coordinate field' whose distance predicates (TOI, standoff, contact) are Euclidean, movement speed is anisotropic — a flanking arc (exactly the maneuvers under discussion) pays a hidden speed tax versus a frontal advance.
detail.root_cause: The L1 normalization was inherited from the grid path's integer step-splitting idiom (units.py:877-880) where it was a rounding scheme, and survived the float migration unexamined.

verdict.evidence: I read units.py:614-616 directly: `mag = abs(dr) + abs(dc)` (L1 norm) then `nar, nac = ar + step*(dr/mag), ac + step*(dc/mag)`. `step` is confirmed to be an intended per-tick scalar speed (line 589: `step = max(0, floor(base*disc_mult)+stance_mod)`, derived from cell_speed/discipline/stance/cavalry-mult), so dividing the direction by the L1 norm makes the resulting displacement's Euclidean length direction-dependent. I ran the arithmetic: step vector Euclidean length = step*sqrt(dr^2+dc^2)/(|dr|+|dc|), giving 1.000*step on-axis, 0.7071*step at 45deg → exactly 29.3% slower (also 20.9% at a 3:1 shallow angle). I confirmed the distance predicates on this path are Euclidean, not L1: the anchor dmin cap uses `math.hypot(...)` under FIELD_MOVEMENT (lines 606-607, 730), and the TOI standoff solver `_pair_toi_scale` (lines 118-154) works in squared-Euclidean distance (`d0r*d0r + d0c*d0c - target*target`) against a Euclidean standoff radius (standoff/standoff_from_reach, lines 65-77). So movement magnitude is L1-normalized while contact/standoff/TOI are Euclidean → genuine anisotropy on the continuous field. The node path (614-616) is the live field-ON path (docstring 554-578 + comment at 870-873 confirm FIELD_MOVEMENT⇒PC_NODE_COHESION routes here). The root cause is corroborated: the legacy grid split at 877-880 (`r_step = round(actual_speed*abs_dr/total)`, `c_step = actual_speed - r_step`, total = abs_dr+abs_dc) is the L1 idiom, and it demonstrably survived the float migration — the sibling float branch at 874-875 uses the identical `total = abs_dr+abs_dc` normalization.
verdict.correction: No correction to the substance — the math, the Euclidean-predicate premise, and the root-cause narrative all hold. Two minor refinements: (1) The most direct evidence that "the L1 normalization survived the float migration unexamined" is actually the float branch at units.py:874-875 (identical `total=abs_dr+abs_dc` normalization on the continuous path), not only the integer grid-split at 877-880 the finding cites; the 877-880 lines are the rounding-scheme ancestor, 874-875 are the un-migrated float copy. (2) Severity "low" is fair: `step` is integer-floored so on short/near-axis moves the effect is small, and the anisotropy is a smooth cos+sin-style penalty rather than a discontinuity — but it is a real, permanent ~29% diagonal speed tax on exactly the flanking-arc maneuvers, so it is not zero-impact.

verified.title: 10 of 14 instruction tokens in ROLE_SPEC are consumed nowhere — the roles built from them are pure labels on every path

detail.title: 10 of 14 instruction tokens in ROLE_SPEC are consumed nowhere — the roles built from them are pure labels on every path
file_line_citations: tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py:727,821,849; tests/sim/mass_battle/core/contact.py:53; tests/sim/mass_battle/resolution.py:77,85 (the complete consumer set: kite, envelop, sweep, brace)
detail.evidence: Package-wide grep: only 'kite', 'envelop', 'sweep' (movement, legacy-only) and 'brace' (combat, PER_CELL-gated) are ever read. 'hold' (as an instruction — distinct from stance 'hold'), 'pin', 'advance', 'loose', 'harass', 'screen', 'pursue', 'charge', 'lure', 'volley', 'shoot_move', 'reserve' have zero consumers — notably 'charge' (the Shock role's whole package; charging is actually keyed off momentum + troop_type=='cavalry') and 'reserve' (Support/Reserve roles do nothing). config.py:153-156 does mark the scaffold INERT by design, but ED-907 L3 'wired' role selection without wiring most packages, so a caller choosing Shock/Anvil/Screen/Pursue/Feint gets shape defaults only.
detail.root_cause: Instruction-to-primitive modulation (pc_formation_design.md §3.5/§9.1) was never landed; role wiring stopped at construction-time defaults. Not a regression, but it materially widens the 'dead instruction' surface the brief asked to bound.

verdict.evidence: I read config.py:173-189 (ROLE_SPEC) and enumerated its instruction tokens programmatically: ROLE_SPEC contains 15 distinct instruction tokens (advance, brace, charge, envelop, harass, hold, kite, loose, lure, pin, pursue, reserve, screen, shoot_move, volley) — NOT 14. An exhaustive package-wide grep for reads of subunit/unit `.instructions` (`'X' in ...instructions`) yields exactly four live consumers: brace (units.py:347, resolution.py:77,85, core/contact.py:53), kite (units.py:727), envelop (units.py:821), and sweep (units.py:849). Of these, sweep is NOT a ROLE_SPEC token — so only 3 ROLE_SPEC tokens (brace, kite, envelop) have live behavioral consumers, and 12 are consumed nowhere: advance, charge, harass, hold, loose, lure, pin, pursue, reserve, screen, shoot_move, volley. I independently confirmed the specific sub-claims: instruction 'hold' has zero consumers (every 'hold' hit is the STANCE, e.g. units.py:579/721, resolution.py:124); 'charge' the Shock-role instruction is dead — charge-shock is keyed off troop_type=='cavalry'/charge_pen (units.py:469) and cavalry velocity/momentum (units.py:762,772, PC_CAVALRY_SPEED_MULT), never off any instruction; 'reserve'/'volley'/'pin'/etc. are dead as instructions (volley hits are weapon keywords/phase names, not instruction reads). config.py:153-156 does mark the scaffold INERT by design, and engine.py:206-215 shows role selection IS wired to shape+instructions defaults at construction. So the qualitative core — most ROLE_SPEC instruction tokens have zero behavioral consumers, leaving the tactical layer of most roles inert — is solidly true and if anything understated (12/15 dead, not 10/14).
verdict.correction: The headline "10 of 14" is numerically wrong on both figures and internally contradicts its own detail (which correctly enumerates 12 dead tokens). ROLE_SPEC has 15 distinct instruction tokens, of which 12 are consumed nowhere and 3 (brace, kite, envelop) have live consumers — i.e. the accurate statement is "12 of 15 ROLE_SPEC instruction tokens are dead." Two further inaccuracies: (1) the cited consumer set "kite, envelop, sweep, brace" folds in 'sweep', which is NOT a ROLE_SPEC token (it only appears in validators.py build-E test scaffolding), so it should not count toward ROLE_SPEC's consumed/total — the real ROLE_SPEC consumers are just brace/kite/envelop. (2) The title's "roles are pure labels on every path" overstates: each role's `shape` field IS consumed (engine.py:211 sets shape=role_spec['shape'], driving Line/Arrowhead/GappedLine geometry), and the brace/envelop/kite instruction packages do have live effects. The finding's own DETAIL is more accurate ("shape defaults only"). Net: the dead-instruction surface is real and slightly larger than claimed, but the specific count and the "pure labels" framing are off.

verified.title: check_orders trigger semantics: a 'tick:N' order fires exactly once per battle (not once per engagement turn), because _order_idx never re-arms across run_battle calls

detail.title: check_orders trigger semantics: a 'tick:N' order fires exactly once per battle (not once per engagement turn), because _order_idx never re-arms across run_battle calls
detail.evidence: Code read + empirical: in the multi-turn H4 runs the wings' stance transitions hold->balanced exactly once (turn 1 tick 4) and instructions gain 'envelop' permanently; snapshots show 'balanced'/'envelop' for turns 2-8 from tick 1. The brief's expectation that the release order re-fires 'once per engagement turn's tick 4, across 8 turns' is wrong — it cannot, by design of the sequential queue. Because the fired behavior persists, this is currently harmless for build_envelopment, but any order whose intent is per-turn (e.g. re-hold each fresh approach) is inexpressible, and this interacts with the reset gap in finding 1 (nothing re-arms between turns).
detail.root_cause: Order queue is a one-shot sequence scoped to the Subunit's lifetime, while triggers are phrased in within-turn tick numbers; the two scopes were never reconciled for multi-turn battles.

verdict.evidence: Read all three cited sites: contact.py:28-58 (while sub._order_idx < len(sub.orders); sub._order_idx += 1 on fire), units.py:303 (_order_idx: int = 0, a persistent Subunit field), orchestration.py:1444-1469 (reset_positions does not touch it). A repo-wide grep of tests/sim/mass_battle shows _order_idx appears in only 4 places (3 in contact.py, 1 def in units.py) — nothing anywhere resets it (not between_turn_recovery, run_battle, or run_multi_turn_battle). Confirmed run_battle (orchestration.py:1043,1063,1117-1118) loops t=1..18 fresh each call and calls check_orders per tick, while run_multi_turn_battle (:1478-1489) calls run_battle once per battle turn on the same persistent unit objects — so t re-arms but _order_idx does not. Independent probe: drove check_orders with a Subunit carrying one 'tick:4' order across 8 turns x 18 ticks -> fired exactly once at (turn 1, tick 4), _order_idx=1, no re-fire turns 2-8. Counterfactual (manually re-arming _order_idx=0 per turn) produced 8 fires, one per turn's tick 4, isolating the persistent index as the cause.
verdict.correction: No correction to the core claim. Minor precision only: strictly, each order in the queue fires once per battle at the first tick (across all engagement turns) where its trigger is satisfied; for tick:N triggers with t reaching 18 in turn 1, that first fire always lands in turn 1, so the finding's wording holds. The peripheral H4 snapshot detail ('balanced'/'envelop' persisting turns 2-8) was not independently re-run via the actual harness, but it is corroborative rather than load-bearing; the underlying mechanism is decisively confirmed. Severity=low and the 'harmless for build_envelopment' framing are sound because the fired behavior is a persistent setattr latch.

synthesis.executive_summary:
You watched the H4 wings walk straight at the enemy because, in the exact configuration the visualizer runs, the movement engine has NO maneuver logic at all. Since the ED-1089 default flip (2026-07-02, config.py:87), every battle runs on the newer "coordinate-field / node" movement path (_node_advance, hierarchy/units.py:554-679). That path does one thing: each tick it steers each body in a straight line toward the enemy's center of mass (units.py:596-616). It never reads a subunit's instructions or role. All four real maneuver behaviors you'd expect — the envelop wrap-to-rear (units.py:821), the sweep flank-march (units.py:849), the overhang-cell wheel (units.py:807), and ranged kiting (units.py:727) — DO exist in the code, but they all live in the OLDER "grid" movement path, which the node path bypasses via an unconditional early return at units.py:719-720. So the maneuvers aren't missing — they're unreachable in the config you're watching. That single fact is the root cause of "the wings just go straight to the opponent," and because the node path is a pure centroid attractor with no file-holding term, they go straight AND converge inward (cols 3/15 collapse toward ~9/13).

Three things to flag prominently. (1) You were told the mounted-archer ruling (ED-1093) was fully executed; it is NOT effective. The patch wired half the chain — a bare mounted_archers unit now gets the "Kite" role (engine.py:201) — but that role never sets unit_type='ranged' (config.py:186; build_army defaults 'melee' at engine.py:216), so the kite branch (units.py:727) and the volley gate (orchestration.py:935) both evaluate False. The unit fires no arrows, does no kiting, gets no speed edge, and inherits generic stats (no registry preset). Triple-broken, on every path. (2) The "144-tick total freeze" anomaly is REFUTED — it does not reproduce at HEAD or three prior commits; the H4 composition moves normally and ends in a draw. The best-fit explanation for the earlier report is a measurement bug (reading each cell's immutable formation-coordinate "id" instead of its live "pos"). But chasing it surfaced a genuine serious bug: the between-turns reset (reset_positions, orchestration.py:1444-1469) is a no-op on the node path, so in a multi-turn battle everyone stays welded at the turn-1 contact line for turns 2-8 (126 of 144 ticks). That IS real. (3) The flagship visualization runs with most combat mechanics compiled out: a separate toggle, PER_CELL, still defaults OFF (config.py:86), so charge shock, brace recoil, cavalry speed, and fatigue are all inert in the run you're watching (all nested under `if PER_CELL:` in orchestration.py:694-756). Whether to flip PER_CELL too is a design call only you can make — ED-1089 flipped only FIELD_MOVEMENT/PC_NODE_COHESION.

Bottom line on the two capabilities you drew (Image 1: both wings arc wide then curl behind; Image 2: two different wings, two different shapes, one striking an interior point between friendly bodies): the current architecture CANNOT express either as-is, but it does NOT need a ground-up rewrite. It needs one new primitive — a per-subunit ordered list of intermediate goal-points, each with a "done-when" predicate, consumed by _node_advance ahead of the straight-line fallback — plus a short chain of prerequisite repairs. Your own legacy envelop code (units.py:814-839) is already a two-state version of exactly this primitive (a synthetic wide-flank goal, recomputed each tick from live enemy extent, that flips to a rear-attack goal once a geometric "have I crossed past the enemy" predicate — your "smaller x than their opponent" test — is satisfied). So the pattern is half-proven in-repo. What matters is sequencing: the reset no-op, the wing-convergence regression, the wheel-facing stall, and re-pointing the acceptance validators at the live path must all land BEFORE the waypoint work, or it will be built on sand and unmeasurable. All node-path edits are byte-exact-safe for the CI grid digests (which pin every toggle OFF and never enter _node_advance), but they WILL change the field digests (bat.py), which require a deliberate, Jordan-signed re-record.

verified_findings.title: The node path is a pure centroid attractor — v12 column-local file-holding exists only in the legacy branch, so multi-subunit wings converge inward during the approach
citations: hierarchy/units.py:596-597, 614-616 (anchor steers straight at target_centroid in both row AND column)

verified_findings.summary: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED. The central high-severity claim holds exactly: on the default node path each subunit anchor is a pure centroid attractor with no file-holding term, so separate wing anchors converge onto the enemy centroid's column (empirically cols 3/15 -> ~9/13 on node vs held files on legacy). The v12 column-local design (documented as a deliberate anti-convergence fix) lives only in the legacy branch, so the default path regressed to pre-v12 behavior — this is why the wings go straight AND inward. One correction to the original finding's title: convergence occurs only BETWEEN separate subunit anchors (wings), NOT within a single GappedLine subunit — a lone gapped block keeps its internal gap open under rigid translation. Restoring a lateral term is a prerequisite for the wide-out leg of Image 1: a wing that has already collapsed inward cannot then arc wide.

verified_findings.title: Formation drift (check_drift shape change) corrupts node state — cells teleport to (0,0), then permanently stack on the anchor
citations: hierarchy/units.py:1288-1301 (check_drift on drift sets shape='Line' and re-keys cell_troops only; ED-1032 comment scopes the fix to cell_troops; _node_pos/_node_rel are never touched)
hierarchy/units.py:547 (_node_cells: _node_pos.get(cid,(0.0,0.0)) — a missing id renders at the battlefield corner)

verified_findings.summary: When a non-Line subunit's discipline degrades below its shape minimum (Arrowhead<4, GappedLine<5, Column<3), check_drift switches shape to 'Line' and rebuilds cell_troops for the new pattern, but leaves the node _node_pos/_node_rel keyed to the OLD pattern's ids. The new Line ids are absent, so cells() returns (0,0) for them (battlefield corner), and the next _node_advance seeds them at the anchor with zero rel offset — permanently stacking ~10 of 25 cells on one point. Verified by probe (Arrowhead tier-3 at discipline 2: 10/25 cells at (0,0) after drift, all 10 collapsed to one anchor point and staying there through 9 advances). Contact, targeting, volley range, and rendering all read the corrupted cells(). ED-1032 fixed the grid re-keying but never included the node state. Must be fixed before any maneuver test where a wing's discipline can degrade mid-maneuver.

verified_findings.title: The envelop/sweep acceptance validators measure the DEAD path under their own documented invocation, and are wired into no CI

This is the measurement instrument for the whole maneuver-repair effort and it is currently pointed at the wrong path — it must be re-pointed (and made an executable gate) before anything can claim the node-path maneuvers work.

verified_findings.title: The node WHEEL facing update degenerates to a zero vector at exact 180-degree reversal — the body-level wheel stalls precisely at the wheel-to-rear case
citations: hierarchy/units.py:632-636 (kw = disc_mult*0.5; lr,lc = fr0 + kw*(ftr-fr0), fc0 + kw*(ftc-fc0); facing updates only `if lmag > 0`)
hierarchy/units.py:584 (disc_mult=1.0 at discipline>=5, giving kw=0.5)
hierarchy/units.py:617-639 (node WHEEL is a body-level FACING rotation, not a path steer — distinct from the legacy overhang-wheel)
verified_findings.summary: The node WHEEL slews facing by a lerp-normalize. At full discipline kw=0.5, so when the desired facing is exactly anti-parallel to the current facing (ftr=-fr0), the lerp yields lr=lc=0, lmag=0, and facing never updates — the wheel stalls at exactly the 180-degree reversal a wheel-to-rear maneuver requires. Arithmetic CONFIRMED by probe (0 at anti-parallel; updates normally at 90 degrees and near-anti-parallel; the zero is specific to kw=0.5 / full discipline — at disc 3-4, kw=0.35 does not vanish). Operational reachability is UNCONFIRMED: in continuous float geometry exact anti-parallel is a knife-edge (measure-zero, self-correcting next tick), so whether a real battle ever hits it is unproven. Regardless, a rear-attack maneuver deliberately drives toward this configuration, so the facing update must be made robust (e.g. slerp / perpendicular nudge) before Image 1's rear strike lands with correct facing.

verified_findings.title: build_army silently discards unknown per-subunit spec keys — the 'speed':'Fast' on the C4/workbench cavalry wings is dropped without error

verified_findings.summary: build_army copies each spec, pops a fixed key set, and never validates leftovers, so 'speed' (and typos like 'instrucions') are silently lost. The impact is worse than a dropped key: speed is unrepresentable at subunit granularity (Subunit has no speed field; it exists only on Unit), and build_envelopment feeds Unit.speed from its own default 'Standard' param, never from the specs — so the C4 wings' Fast pursuit is lost end-to-end with no warning. Verified by probe (build succeeds silently; Unit.speed stays 'Standard'; Subunit.of_type(speed='Fast') would raise, proving the fixed-key pop is what avoids a crash by dropping the key). Fix is a strict unknown-key check (fail-loud, matching role_allowed) plus a decision on whether per-subunit speed should be supported at all — note a naive strict check would immediately reject the existing 'speed' key, so the two must be resolved together.

verified_findings.title: Field-path movement is L1-normalized — diagonal moves are ~29% slower than axis-aligned, a hidden speed tax on exactly the flanking arcs under discussion
citations: hierarchy/units.py:614-616 (mag = abs(dr)+abs(dc); nar = ar + step*(dr/mag) — L1 normalization of the step direction)
hierarchy/units.py:874-875 (the un-migrated float copy on the legacy path uses the identical total=abs_dr+abs_dc idiom; 877-880 is its integer grid-split ancestor)
hierarchy/units.py:606-607, 730 and _pair_toi_scale at 118-154 (distance predicates on this path are Euclidean — math.hypot / squared-Euclidean standoff)
verified_findings.summary: The step vector step*(dr/mag, dc/mag) with mag=|dr|+|dc| has Euclidean length step on an axis but step/sqrt(2) ~ 0.707*step at 45 degrees — a permanent ~29% diagonal speed tax (20.9% at a 3:1 shallow angle). Because contact/standoff/TOI on this path are all Euclidean, movement magnitude is anisotropic: a wing tracing a wide arc pays a hidden tax versus a frontal advance. CONFIRMED by arithmetic and code read. Severity low (step is integer-floored, so on short near-axis moves the effect is small and it is a smooth penalty, not a discontinuity), but it distorts precisely the arc maneuvers Images 1/2 require. Root cause: the L1 normalization is an un-migrated inheritance from the grid path's integer step-splitting idiom. Fix (switch to Euclidean normalization) changes all field battles, so it needs a full field re-baseline — defer unless the arc maneuvers prove sensitive to it.

verified_findings.title: 12 of 15 ROLE_SPEC instruction tokens are consumed nowhere — most roles are shape defaults only, with inert tactical packages

verified_findings.title: A 'tick:N' order fires exactly once per battle, not once per engagement turn — _order_idx never re-arms across run_battle calls
citations: core/contact.py:28-58 (while sub._order_idx < len(sub.orders); _order_idx += 1 on fire)
