fix_plan.title: Fix check_drift node-state re-keying (corruption bug — do first, independent and low-risk)
fix_plan.approach: In check_drift (units.py:1288-1301), when a subunit drifts to shape='Line', extend the existing ED-1032 cell_troops re-key so the node state is re-keyed to the new pattern's ids too. Build the new id set from _oriented(a) (already computed for cell_troops at :1299) and rebuild a._node_pos and a._node_rel for those ids: seed each new id's _node_rel from the Line pattern's relational offset (the same offset _init_node_state computes at construction) and place each new id's _node_pos at anchor+rel so no cell renders at (0,0) or collapses onto the anchor. Preserve the anchor/_node_anchor. The cleanest implementation is to factor out (or reuse) the _init_node_state relational-seeding logic keyed on the post-drift shape rather than re-deriving offsets inline.
fix_plan.byte_exact_considerations: This block only executes when a subunit actually drifts (eff_discipline < MIN_DISCIPLINE and shape != 'Line'), and only touches node state (_node_pos/_node_rel), which the OFF grid path never reads. CI grid digests (test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py, _PINNED_OFF) are untouched. Field digests change ONLY for scenarios where a subunit drifts; identify any bat.py digest scenario that reaches drift and re-record those specifically (most will not drift and stay identical).
fix_plan.risk_notes: Must reuse the existing relational-offset derivation, not invent new offsets (evidentiary-primitive discipline). Verify with the finding-5 probe (Arrowhead tier-3 at discipline 2): after check_drift + one _node_advance, zero cells at (0,0) and no two cells sharing one point.

fix_plan.title: Teach reset_positions to reset node state (multi-turn re-approach) — gated on a Jordan re-arm ruling
fix_plan.approach: In reset_positions (orchestration.py:1444-1469), after setting starting_position from each subunit's _spawn_position, re-initialize node state for each subunit so cells() (which reads _node_pos) actually returns them to spawn: call the subunit's _init_node_state()-equivalent seeded from the reset starting_position, resetting _node_pos, _node_anchor, _node_rel, _node_facing, _node_facing0, and any _speed_accum. Expose a small Subunit method (e.g. reset_node_state(start_row)) in units.py rather than reaching into private fields from orchestration. SEPARATELY, per the Jordan ruling on multi-turn semantics (see recommended_sequencing), decide whether stance/_order_idx/instructions also re-arm here; if yes, reset them in the same method — this also resolves finding 12's re-arm gap.
fix_plan.byte_exact_considerations: reset_positions is called only from run_multi_turn_battle/run_multi_unit_battle (the 'multi' paths); single battles never call it. Grid digests (toggles OFF) are unaffected because the node fields are never read on the OFF path. Field digests for ALL multi-turn scenarios (bat.py:142-161 unit_field/cell_field) WILL change — turns 2-8 now genuinely re-approach instead of grinding at the contact line. This is a deliberate, Jordan-signed re-record, not a regression.
fix_plan.risk_notes: Biggest re-verification burden in the plan: gauge_mb multi-mode historical bands were presumably last validated on the grid path and need a full field-path re-run once turns 2+ re-approach. The pre-existing platform-dependent RNG desync in the 'cell' grid digest (test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py:32-50) will collide with any re-baselining — coordinate with that. Do NOT re-arm command state until Jordan rules on turn semantics; landing the positional reset without a decision would produce undefined turn-2 posture.

fix_plan.title: Wire ED-1093 mounted-archer role to unit_type='ranged' via data (no entity special-casing)
fix_plan.approach: Add a unit_type field to the relevant ROLE_SPEC entries (config.py:173-189) — at minimum 'Kite', and consider 'Harass'/'VolleyLine'/'Skirmish' for consistency — then in build_army (engine.py:206-217) derive unit_type from role_spec when the spec does not supply an explicit unit_type (explicit spec value still wins, matching every other precedence rule in the function). Concretely: ROLE_SPEC['Kite'] gains "unit_type":"ranged"; build_army's kw dict uses sp.pop('unit_type', role_spec.get('unit_type','melee') if role_spec else 'melee'). This makes a bare mounted_archers spec resolve to unit_type='ranged', enabling the volley gate (orchestration.py:935) immediately. The exact schema location (role-level unit_type vs a troop-type ranged flag in registry.py) is Jordan-vetoable — pick per his ruling.
fix_plan.byte_exact_considerations: Changes behavior only for specs that resolve to a role carrying unit_type (Kite etc.). Verify no bat.py digest scenario builds via role='Kite'/mounted_archers (the default digests use build_unit, so likely none — confirm by grep before re-recording). If none touch it, all digests stay byte-identical.
fix_plan.risk_notes: This fixes only the DATA half of ED-1093 (unit fires arrows/volleys). The STEERING half — actual kiting/standoff regulation — still does not exist on the node path (finding 1); on the default path a ranged unit will still close to melee until the waypoint/standoff work lands. Flag to Jordan that this step alone gives volley fire but not kiting. Do not add a TROOP_TYPE_STATS/TROOP_TYPE_REACH preset with invented magnitudes for mounted_archers (evidentiary-primitive discipline — TROOP_TYPE_REACH stays empty); a stats preset, if wanted, is a separate ledger-backed decision.

fix_plan.title: Restore a lateral file-holding term on the node path (port v12 column-local targeting)
fix_plan.approach: In _node_advance (units.py:595-616), add a per-subunit lateral-doctrine term so a wide-deployed wing does not converge its column onto target_centroid during a plain advance. The minimal faithful port of the legacy v12 mechanism (units.py:789-801) is to give the anchor a column target of its OWN deployment column (from _spawn_position / starting_position) while keeping the row target = target_centroid[0], rather than steering column toward target_centroid[1]. Equivalently, decompose the anchor step into a row component aimed at the enemy and a column component that holds the deployment file unless a maneuver goal overrides it. Keep this subordinate to the waypoint layer (step 7) so a maneuver can still command lateral motion.
fix_plan.byte_exact_considerations: Node-path only (below the units.py:719-720 early return is legacy; this edit is inside _node_advance itself, which is the node path) — grid OFF digests untouched. Field digests for any multi-subunit / wide-deployment scenario change (wings now hold files); single-subunit-centered scenarios where deployment column == centroid column are unaffected. Re-record affected field digests deliberately.
fix_plan.risk_notes: Preserves formation SHAPE via the existing cohesion relax (units.py:653-658); this term governs the ANCHOR's column only. Prerequisite for Image 1's wide-out leg — a wing that already collapsed inward cannot arc wide. Confirm empirically that wings deployed at cols 3/15 hold their files on the node path after this change (matching the legacy grid), per finding 4's probe.

fix_plan.title: Make the node WHEEL facing update robust at 180-degree reversal
fix_plan.approach: Replace the lerp-normalize facing update (units.py:632-636) with an update that does not vanish when current and desired facings are anti-parallel — e.g. a spherical-lerp (slerp) toward the desired unit vector, or a fixed-angle rotation of _node_facing toward ftr/ftc by min(wheel_rate_angle, remaining_angle), so the magnitude is never lost. Keep the discipline-gated rate (kw derives from disc_mult) so disciplined formations still wheel faster; only the degenerate-zero case changes. When exactly anti-parallel, break the tie by rotating a fixed small angle in a deterministic direction (e.g. toward the side the target lies on) rather than emitting the zero vector.
fix_plan.byte_exact_considerations: Node-path only. This changes facing (cell_facing_vec / octagon angle) whenever a body actually reverses facing; head-on engagements (f stays ~ f0) are unchanged because the rotation stays near identity. Field digests change only for scenarios that reach a large facing reversal — re-record those. Grid OFF digests untouched.
fix_plan.risk_notes: Do not introduce a new magnitude for the wheel rate — derive the per-tick angular step from the existing disc_mult-based kw (evidentiary-primitive discipline). Reachability of the exact-180 stall in real battles is unconfirmed (knife-edge), but a rear-attack maneuver deliberately drives toward it, so this must land before Image 1's rear strike or the wing arrives facing the wrong way.

fix_plan.title: Re-point the maneuver acceptance validators at the node path and make them an executable gate (establish red target BEFORE waypoint work)
fix_plan.approach: In validators.py, pin FIELD_MOVEMENT/PC_NODE_COHESION explicitly (do not rely on ambient defaults) so V-ENVELOP/V-SWEEP measure the intended path; ideally run BOTH modes (legacy-grid arm as a regression, node arm as the live target). Update the documented Run: line (validators.py:23) to pin all three toggles. Add an executable pytest under tests/valoria/ that invokes the node-path maneuver acceptance (envelop reaches behind the defender; sweep displaces laterally) so it runs in CI. Land this step with the node-path assertions initially RED (they cannot pass until step 7), documenting the target the waypoint work must hit.
fix_plan.byte_exact_considerations: Test/validator code only — no engine behavior change, so no digest impact. The new pytest must set toggles via env at import time (the module binds toggle values at import), matching the existing test's _PINNED_OFF discipline.
fix_plan.risk_notes: This is the measurement instrument for the entire maneuver effort (finding 7) — without it, 'the maneuvers work now' is unverifiable and the prior 'Stage C.4 passed' mistake repeats. Keep the legacy-grid arm as a permanent regression so the byte-exact-OFF maneuver path stays green independently.

fix_plan.title: Add the waypoint primitive to _node_advance — the actual Image 1 / Image 2 capability
files_to_change: tests/sim/mass_battle/config.py (toggle only, if a new gate is wanted)
fix_plan.approach: Add a per-subunit ordered list of (goal-function, completion-predicate) pairs and a consumption step in _node_advance that, before falling back to target_centroid (units.py:595-616), resolves the current active goal to a point and steers the anchor toward THAT point. Model it directly on the legacy envelop 2-state machine (units.py:814-839): goals are FUNCTIONS of live geometry re-evaluated every tick (never frozen coordinates — a frozen goal goes stale against a moving enemy), and completion predicates reuse the existing Order-trigger vocabulary (tick / enemy_range / ally_at) PLUS one new 'crossed-past' predicate = the legacy _past test (my advance-axis coordinate beyond the enemy's row extent — Jordan's 'smaller x than their opponent'). Image 1 = per-wing list [wide-flank point, behind-enemy point gated on crossed-past, terminal nearest-rear-cell]; Image 2 = DIFFERENT lists per wing, where the right wing's terminal goal is computed from ALLIED geometry (a point between center and left wing) — reuse the Order.waypoint_ref / 'ally_at:D' allied-reference idiom rather than growing a new dialect. Goal points must maintain clearance > standoff (reuse the legacy envelop's frontage+2 margin, units.py:827) and respect BATTLEFIELD_SIZE clamping (units.py:657-658) so 'wide' never emits out-of-bounds. Wire the goal lists through build_army/build_envelopment as spec data.
fix_plan.byte_exact_considerations: Node-path only; the goal layer must be INERT (empty list -> identical to today's pure centroid attractor) so every non-maneuvering scenario is byte-identical on the field path and every OFF scenario is byte-identical on the grid. Field digests for maneuvering scenarios change (by design) and re-record. If a new toggle gates the waypoint layer, default it OFF-preserving (i.e. empty goal list already yields old behavior, so a toggle is optional).
fix_plan.risk_notes: Depends on steps 2 (multi-turn reset — maneuvers are meaningless if turns 2+ start welded), 4 (lateral term — wings must not converge before the arc begins), 5 (wheel fix — rear attacks need correct facing), and 6 (acceptance gate — to prove it). All clearance/margin/rear-offset magnitudes must derive from existing ratified quantities (standoff_from_reach, COL_WIDTH, enemy extent, the +2 frontage margin) — invent nothing (evidentiary-primitive discipline). Open design gate: what the body should FACE during a wide-out transit leg (movement goal vs threat) — the current WHEEL conflates steering and facing target; needs a Jordan ruling before finalizing facing policy.

synthesis.recommended_sequencing:
The two general maneuver capabilities (Image 1 wide-arc-then-wrap, Image 2 asymmetric per-wing shapes with an interior strike point) are the goal, but they sit at the top of a dependency chain. Nothing about them is buildable until the default path can (a) run a multi-turn approach without welding at the contact line, (b) keep wide wings from collapsing inward before a maneuver begins, (c) reverse facing without stalling, and (d) be measured. Concretely:

DECISION GATES (Jordan rulings, must precede the code they gate):
1. Multi-turn re-arm semantics: does each engagement turn re-hold stance / re-arm orders / reset instructions for a fresh approach, or do turn-1 releases latch for the whole battle? This governs BOTH the reset_positions fix (fix step 2) and the once-per-battle order behavior (finding 12) — resolve it first.
2. Where unit_type='ranged' lives for the Kite role (role-level field vs troop-type ranged flag) — gates fix step 3 (ED-1093).
3. Waypoint transit facing policy: during a wide-out leg, does the body face its movement goal, the enemy, or slew between them phase-by-phase? Gates the facing half of fix step 7. The current WHEEL conflates steering target and facing target, and Image 1's arc makes them differ for many ticks.
4. Should PER_CELL flip to '1' alongside ED-1089's field flip (finding 8)? Independent of the maneuver chain but decides what the flagship visualization actually shows — resolve before demoing any maneuver to avoid showing a run with charge/brace/cavalry-speed compiled out.

CODE SEQUENCE (dependency-ordered):
- Land fix step 1 (check_drift node fix) first — independent, low-risk, and it prevents a discipline-degrading wing from corrupting cells() mid-maneuver, which would otherwise poison every downstream maneuver test.
- Land fix steps 2, 4, 5 next, in that order — these are the three prerequisites that make a node-path maneuver physically meaningful: multi-turn re-approach (2), wings that hold their files (4), and a wheel that can reverse (5). Fix step 3 (ED-1093 data half) can land in parallel here; it is orthogonal but part of 'make the roles real.'
- Land fix step 6 (re-point validators + node-path acceptance gate, initially RED) before step 7 — this is the instrument that proves the capability and prevents repeating the 'Stage C.4 passed on the dead path' mistake (finding 7). Establish the red target, then implement.
- Land fix step 7 (the waypoint primitive) — this is where Images 1 and 2 become expressible. It turns the step-6 assertions green.
- Fix step 8 (hardening) is parallelizable afterward; sequence the L1->L2 normalization last and only on evidence, as it has the widest field-digest blast radius.

RE-VERIFICATION BURDEN to plan for (do not treat as free): fix steps 2, 4, 5, 7 all change the bat.py field digests (unit_field/cell_field) — every one requires a deliberate, Jordan-signed field re-record, because those digests currently embed the broken behavior. gauge_mb multi-mode historical bands were presumably last validated on the grid path and need a full field-path re-run once turns 2+ genuinely re-approach. The pre-existing platform-dependent RNG desync in the 'cell' grid digest (test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py:32-50) is unfixed and will collide with any re-baselining in this area — schedule it into the same re-record pass. Throughout, the byte-exact invariant is safe by construction: every engine edit here is inside _node_advance / the node path, which the CI grid digests never enter (they pin every toggle OFF), and no fix may introduce a new magnitude — all clearance, rear-offset, and margin values must derive from existing ratified quantities (standoff_from_reach, COL_WIDTH, enemy extent, the legacy envelop's +2 frontage margin, BATTLEFIELD_SIZE), with TROOP_TYPE_REACH left empty.
